# Cronograma Creator — AI Agent Instructions

> Version: 3.0
> URL: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/
> Schema: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/state.schema.json
> Well-known: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/cronograma-ai.json

Cronograma Creator is an interactive Gantt chart builder that runs entirely in the browser.
AI agents can create, load, and export schedules **without any human interaction**, using three approaches described below.

---

## QUICK START — Generate a share URL without a browser

This is the fastest method. No browser or API needed.

```javascript
// 1. Build the state object
const state = {
  "title": "Q2 Roadmap",
  "startMonth": "0",        // 0 = January
  "endMonth": "5",          // 5 = June
  "theme": "oscuro",        // oscuro | claro | moderno | gris
  "lang": "en",             // en | es
  "projects": [
    {
      "name": "Design",
      "color": "#5B8CC8",
      "tasksByRow": [
        [
          {
            "name": "Research",
            "startWeek": 1,
            "duration": 3,
            "isMilestone": false,
            "textPosition": "outside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "name": "Wireframes",
            "startWeek": 4,
            "duration": 4,
            "isMilestone": false,
            "textPosition": "inside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ]
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "Development",
      "color": "#2E9E58",
      "tasksByRow": [
        [
          {
            "name": "Backend API",
            "startWeek": 5,
            "duration": 8,
            "isMilestone": false,
            "textPosition": "inside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "name": "Frontend",
            "startWeek": 9,
            "duration": 6,
            "isMilestone": false,
            "textPosition": "inside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "name": "Launch",
            "startWeek": 15,
            "duration": 1,
            "isMilestone": true,
            "textPosition": "outside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  ]
};

// 2. Encode it
const hash = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(state))));

// 3. Build the URL
const url = `https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s=${hash}`;

// → Share this URL. It opens the schedule instantly in any browser.
```

**Python equivalent:**
```python
import json, base64
encoded = base64.b64encode(json.dumps(state, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')).decode('utf-8')
url = f"https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s={encoded}"
```

> Note: Python uses standard base64 without `unescape/encodeURIComponent`. Use UTF-8 encoding.

---

## STATE SCHEMA

Full JSON Schema available at: `state.schema.json`

### Top-level fields

| Field | Type | Required | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | yes | Any text |
| `startMonth` | string or int | yes | 0–11 (0=Jan, 11=Dec) |
| `endMonth` | string or int | yes | 0–11, must be >= startMonth |
| `theme` | string | yes | `"oscuro"` `"claro"` `"moderno"` `"gris"` |
| `lang` | string | yes | `"es"` `"en"` |
| `projects` | array | yes | See below |

### Project fields

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes | Group label |
| `color` | string | yes | Hex color e.g. `"#5B8CC8"` |
| `compact` | boolean | no | Smaller row height for all tasks |
| `tasksByRow` | array of arrays | yes | Each inner array = one visual row |

### Task fields

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes | Task label |
| `startWeek` | integer ≥ 1 | yes | 1 = first week of startMonth |
| `duration` | integer ≥ 1 | yes | Duration in weeks |
| `isMilestone` | boolean | no | Diamond shape; use duration=1 |
| `textPosition` | string | no | `"inside"` or `"outside"` |
| `compact` | boolean | no | Smaller row height |
| `completed` | boolean | no | Strikethrough style |
| `color` | string or null | no | Hex override; null = project color |

### Default color palette (cycle order)
`#5B8CC8` `#8A58B0` `#C97838` `#2E9E58` `#C8A028` `#B84848` `#2A9080` `#3A80B0`

---

## JAVASCRIPT API (browser / headless)

When the page is open in a browser, use `window.CronogramaAPI`:

```javascript
// Load a schedule
CronogramaAPI.createFromJSON(stateObjectOrJSONString);
// or
CronogramaAPI.setState(stateObject);

// Get the share URL
const url = CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl();       // also: exportAsURL()

// Get a PNG image (base64 data URL)
const png = await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl();  // also: exportAsImage()

// Read current state
const state = CronogramaAPI.getState();

// Validate a state object before loading
const { valid, errors } = CronogramaAPI.validateState(myState);

// Run self-test
const results = await CronogramaAPI.selfTest();
// → { success: true, passed: [...], failed: [...], summary: "9 passed, 0 failed" }
```

### Headless workflow (Playwright / Puppeteer / browser-use)

```javascript
const page = await browser.newPage();

// Option A: load a pre-built state URL
await page.goto('https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s=<base64hash>');

// Option B: inject state after loading
await page.goto('https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/?mode=agent');
await page.evaluate((state) => CronogramaAPI.createFromJSON(state), myState);

// Get share URL
const shareUrl = await page.evaluate(() => CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl());

// Get PNG image as data URL
const imageDataUrl = await page.evaluate(async () => await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl());
// imageDataUrl = "data:image/png;base64,..." → decode bytes for the actual PNG

// Run self-test
const testResults = await page.evaluate(async () => await CronogramaAPI.selfTest());
```

---

## URL PARAMETERS

All parameters work together. Example: `?mode=agent&autoexport=url#s=<hash>`

### `?mode=agent`

Activates agent mode:
- Confirms suppressed (auto-approved)
- Alerts logged to console instead of blocking
- Deterministic render (no animations)
- Console logs prefixed with `[CronogramaAI]`

### `?autoexport=url`

On page load, generates the share URL and writes to `#autoexport-result`:
```json
{ "success": true, "type": "url", "data": "https://.../#s=..." }
```
Also shows the URL visually at the top of the page.

### `?autoexport=image`

On page load, generates the PNG and writes to `#autoexport-result`:
```json
{ "success": true, "type": "image", "data": "data:image/png;base64,..." }
```
Also triggers a file download (suppressed in agent mode).

### `?autoexport=json`

On page load, writes the full state JSON to `#autoexport-result`:
```json
{ "success": true, "type": "json", "data": { "title": "...", "projects": [...] } }
```

---

## DOM ELEMENTS ALWAYS AVAILABLE

After every render, these DOM elements are updated automatically:

### `#app-test-state`

```javascript
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.shareUrl   // share URL string
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.state      // full state as JSON string
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.imageReady // "true" after first render
document.getElementById('app-test-state').innerText          // JSON: { state, shareUrl, imageReady }
```

### `#autoexport-result`

Present after any `?autoexport=` param:
```javascript
const result = JSON.parse(document.getElementById('autoexport-result').dataset.result);
// { success: true, type: "url"|"image"|"json", data: ... }
```

---

## COMPLETE WORKED EXAMPLE (copy-paste)

Goal: create a 6-month software project schedule and get a share URL.

```javascript
// JavaScript — runs in browser console or Playwright/Puppeteer

const state = {
  title: "App Launch Plan",
  startMonth: "0", endMonth: "5",
  theme: "oscuro", lang: "en",
  projects: [
    {
      name: "Planning", color: "#5B8CC8",
      tasksByRow: [
        [{ name: "Requirements", startWeek: 1, duration: 2, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Architecture", startWeek: 3, duration: 2, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Kickoff", startWeek: 5, duration: 1, isMilestone: true, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }]
      ]
    },
    {
      name: "Development", color: "#2E9E58",
      tasksByRow: [
        [{ name: "Sprint 1", startWeek: 5, duration: 4, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "inside", compact: false, completed: true, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Sprint 2", startWeek: 9, duration: 4, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "inside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Sprint 3", startWeek: 13, duration: 4, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "inside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }]
      ]
    },
    {
      name: "QA & Launch", color: "#C97838",
      tasksByRow: [
        [{ name: "Testing", startWeek: 17, duration: 3, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Launch", startWeek: 20, duration: 1, isMilestone: true, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: "#B84848" }]
      ]
    }
  ]
};

// Option A: get URL directly (no browser needed)
const hash = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(state))));
const shareUrl = `https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s=${hash}`;
console.log(shareUrl);

// Option B: use the API in browser
CronogramaAPI.createFromJSON(state);
const shareUrl2 = CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl();
const imageDataUrl = await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl();
```

---

## VALIDATION

Always validate before loading to catch errors early:

```javascript
const { valid, errors } = CronogramaAPI.validateState(myState);
if (!valid) {
  console.error('Invalid state:', errors);
  // errors example: ["projects[0].tasksByRow[0][0].startWeek must be integer >= 1"]
}
```

---

## SELF-TEST

Verify the API is working correctly:

```javascript
const results = await CronogramaAPI.selfTest();
// {
//   success: true,
//   summary: "9 passed, 0 failed",
//   passed: [{ test: "createFromJSON", detail: undefined }, ...],
//   failed: []
// }
```

---

## NOTES

- `startWeek` is 1-based. Week 1 = first Monday of `startMonth`.
- Tasks in the same inner array of `tasksByRow` share a horizontal row and can overlap visually.
- `color: null` on a task means it inherits the project color.
- The app auto-saves to `localStorage`. Use `?mode=agent` to avoid side-effects.
- All operations are client-side. No server calls. No authentication required.
